title: OWL
abstract: OWL is a W3C ontology language with formally defined semantics for classes, properties, individuals and constraints on the Semantic Web.
description: A technology record for formal ontology publication and reasoning.

sources.title: OWL 2 Web Ontology Language Document Overview

roleInEcosystem: OWL provides a formal reference for ontology semantics, constraints and inference beyond lightweight taxonomies.
bodyHtml:
Role

OWL formalizes domain classes, properties and constraints with semantics that can support automated reasoning.

Use

It is a reference for cases where Electronic Artefacts needs stronger interoperability or inference than the current pragmatic entity schema provides.

References

See W3C OWL 2, RDF and Ontology.
